blockers.title: Directory exposes a non-approved profile field
blockers.evidence: Spec D11 limits directory fields to name, photo, headline, country, career stage, function/role, sectors and looking_for, with private fields excluded (specs/panel-experience.spec.md:94-99). The server type and payload include bio (convex/directory.ts:31-42 and 135-148), and the UI searches and renders it (src/portal/views/DirectoryView.tsx:35-45, 89-95, 194-198). Migrated members get bio from legacy_bio (convex/members.ts:831-844), so historical profile copy can be exposed to all eligible directory viewers.
blockers.required_fix: Remove bio from the directory query payload, client search, and member card. Add a regression test proving directory rows never include bio or legacy_bio.

blockers.title: Admin and super-admin authorization are collapsed
blockers.evidence: Stage 0 defines admin as Mervat plus backup approver for fallback actions/review queues, and super_admin as Issam with additional certificate revoke/re-issue, erasure, and config powers (/Users/ismac/Documents/Projects/WAI/02 Platform/02 Stage 0 - Technical Design (Public Site & Portal).md:84-95). This slice implements only requireSuperAdmin against SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS (convex/lib/adminAuth.ts:6-13 and 84-93), then gates ordinary admin surfaces with it, for example events (convex/admin/events.ts:82-87), members (convex/admin/members.ts:135-144), partners (convex/admin/partners.ts:66-70), and opportunities (convex/admin/opportunities.ts:478-483). Certificates are also super-admin gated (convex/admin/certificates.ts:42-55). In practice, named admins cannot operate required admin queues unless granted super-admin powers.
blockers.required_fix: Introduce separate admin and super-admin server-side grants. Gate fallback/review/admin-console operations with admin-or-super-admin, keep certificate revoke/re-issue and other super-only actions behind super-admin, and add tests for both positive admin access and denied certificate powers.

blockers.title: Single-winner opportunities can record multiple winners
blockers.evidence: The vault defines single-winner placement as the same opportunity flow with one winner (/Users/ismac/Documents/Projects/WAI/02 Platform/02 Scholarship & Opportunity Workflow (Design).md:28-36), and the spec includes the single_winner opportunity type (specs/panel-experience.spec.md:57-64). recordResult patches any received/shortlisted application to won without checking existing winners (convex/admin/opportunities.ts:472-508). decideOpportunity only checks that no applications remain received/shortlisted, not that a single_winner opportunity has at most one winner (convex/admin/opportunities.ts:553-604).
blockers.required_fix: For type single_winner, reject recording won when another non-withdrawn application is already won, and make decideOpportunity enforce the winner cardinality before publishing results. Cover this with server tests.

non_blocking_notes: No member-facing em dash blocker found in the reviewed added portal/admin strings. The generic panel kit still contains unused "coming soon" support, but I found no caller setting soon:true.

spec_compliance.evidence: Directory payload and UI include bio despite the spec's allowed field list omitting it.
All admin surfaces are super-admin only, collapsing the admin and super_admin roles defined in the vault.
The code permits multiple won applications for a single_winner opportunity.

spec_compliance.criterion: Brand lock: no em dashes in member-facing added strings
spec_compliance.evidence: Reviewed added member/admin UI strings for em dash usage; no shipped member-facing blocker found.

summary: Gate 4 fails. The slice has a directory data exposure outside the approved field tier, an authorization model that does not match the vault's admin/super-admin separation, and missing single-winner integrity enforcement.
